Title: Efficient proteome-wide identification of transcription factors targeting Glu-1: A case study for functional validation of TaB3-2A1 in wheat

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage reserves important for grain nutritional/processing quality and carbon storage.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TaB3-2A1 represses Glu-1 and other seed storage protein accumulation, lowers grain protein content, and increases starch content; it also affects heading date, plant height, and thousand kernel weight.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional regulation of seed storage protein biosynthesis and coordination of starch biosynthesis during grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in developing grains among its homoeologs; widely expressed with highest levels in young spikes and developing seeds; transcript peaks at 5 dpa then declines.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Widely expressed with highest levels in young spikes and developing seeds of wheat cv. Fielder; temporally opposite to Glu-1 during grain development.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent integrator that shifts endosperm storage reserve balance from protein toward starch and directly represses Glu-1 promoter activity.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Glu-1Bx14; Glu-1By15; Glu-1Dx2; Glu-1Dy12; other SSP genes; TaAGPL3; TaAGPS2; TaGBSSI/TaWx1; TaSUS1; TaSUS5
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for wheat overexpression; Glu-1Dx2 promoter/CCRM1-1 used in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for marker-assisted selection and engineering of grain composition/yield trade-offs; Hap1 associated with lower protein but higher starch, plant height, grain weight, and likely yield. Editing or allele deployment should consider end-use quality penalties from reduced gluten proteins.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to the REMD subfamily of B3 TFs. Hap1 was positively selected in modern elite cultivars. Loss-of-function validation was proposed but not performed.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grains analyzed mainly at 10 dpa; greenhouse 16 h light/8 h dark, 10–22°C for wheat transformation studies; field evaluation in Shandong/Henan/Anhui.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B3 transcription factor; REMD subfamily; B3 DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses Glu-1 promoter activity via binding CCRM1-1; transcriptome shows downregulation of SSP genes and upregulation of starch synthesis-related genes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced HMW-GS, LMW-GS and total seed protein may impair processing quality despite increases in starch and grain weight; increased plant height may affect lodging risk.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA pull-down plus LC-MS; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; transactivation assays in tobacco and Arabidopsis protoplasts; wheat overexpression; RNA-seq; protein and starch quantification; haplotype-trait association
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of Glu-1; indirect/overall activation of starch biosynthesis-related gene expression in overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaB3-2A1 was identified by DNA pull-down plus LC-MS as a CCRM1-1-binding B3 TF and directly binds the Glu-1 promoter. 2. It acts as a transcriptional repressor of Glu-1, reducing HMW-GS, other SSP, and total seed protein in wheat grains. 3. TaB3-2A1 overexpression increases starch content and upregulates starch/sucrose metabolism genes, indicating coordination of carbon-nitrogen reserve allocation. 4. Natural haplotype Hap1 is associated with lower protein and higher starch/TKW and has been positively selected in breeding.
